decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Use biology's unifying ideas and elementary evidence reasoning to explain living systems across scales.

chapters.title: Patterns of life and the limits of definitions

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Use units, ratios, algebra, logarithms, functions, graphs, probability, and estimation in biological contexts.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Reason from atomic interactions, water, pH, concentration, energetics, and macromolecular chemistry to biological behavior.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Describe variation, quantify uncertainty, choose basic analyses, and make bounded inferences from biological data.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Explain how membranes, compartments, structures, transport, signaling, division, and energy transformations make cells work.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Connect biomolecular structure, enzymes, thermodynamics, pathways, regulation, and compartmentation to metabolic function.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Predict and interpret inheritance using chromosomes, recombination, probability, pedigrees, variation, and gene interactions.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Explain genome replication, expression, regulation, mutation, repair, and molecular experiments without treating information flow as a rigid slogan.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Use population processes, selection, drift, gene flow, speciation, phylogeny, and evidence to explain biological change and diversity.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Analyze organisms, populations, communities, ecosystems, landscapes, and the biosphere across space and time.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Interpret the tree of life, organismal diversity, classification, form, life cycles, and natural-history evidence.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Plan ethical, safe, reproducible laboratory and field investigations; document methods; evaluate literature; and communicate results.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Use a first programming environment, structured biological data, databases, reproducible workflows, and simple simulations.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Explain microbial and viral diversity, growth, metabolism, genetics, ecology, host interactions, and methods.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Connect plant anatomy, transport, photosynthesis, nutrition, signaling, development, reproduction, defense, and environmental response.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Use gradients, mass balance, feedback, structure-function, and comparative evidence to explain integrated animal function.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Explain how cells generate form through specification, patterning, morphogenesis, growth, regeneration, and environmental interaction.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Relate neural signaling, circuits, sensory and motor systems, plasticity, and cognition across levels of organization.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Analyze behavior through mechanism, development, function, evolutionary history, communication, learning, and social systems.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Explain barrier, innate, and adaptive defense; recognition; signaling; tolerance; memory; and immune-system failure.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Evaluate biodiversity loss, population viability, climate and land-use change, conservation evidence, restoration, and human dimensions.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Interpret sequence, variation, expression, annotation, comparative genomics, pipelines, databases, and reproducible genomic evidence.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Build, analyze, fit, and critique network, dynamical, stochastic, and multiscale models of biological systems.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Infer molecular mechanism from forces, energy landscapes, macromolecular structure, dynamics, and complementary measurement methods.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Design and evaluate engineered biological parts, circuits, cells, products, measurement standards, risks, and societal tradeoffs.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Integrate physiology, immunity, genetics, environment, development, and evolution to explain bounded human disease mechanisms.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Use genome-scale variation and comparative data to test demographic, selective, phylogenomic, and molecular-evolution hypotheses.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Analyze microbial communities, interactions, biogeochemistry, host associations, horizontal transfer, and community evolution.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Explain morphological innovation and constraint by comparing developmental mechanisms, gene networks, life histories, and phylogenies.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Combine monitoring data, hierarchical models, uncertainty, scenarios, and decision analysis for ecological forecasts and conservation.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Integrate measurement, inference, control, design-build-test cycles, multiscale models, and responsible engineering of living systems.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Critique and integrate single-cell and spatial measurements, preprocessing, uncertainty, multimodal inference, and biological validation.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Read frontier literature and design research programs for programmable cells, multicellular systems, biomaterials, and biomanufacturing.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Synthesize organismal, microbial, ecological, remote-sensing, and Earth-system evidence about biosphere responses and feedbacks.

decks.subject: biology
decks.description: Evaluate competing hypotheses and evidence for prebiotic chemistry, early cells, metabolic and genetic systems, and major evolutionary transitions.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Reason fluently with whole, signed, rational, decimal, percent, proportional, and estimated quantities in authentic contexts.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Translate among situations, expressions, equations, inequalities, tables, and graphs and solve elementary algebraic models.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Reason with Euclidean shape, congruence, similarity, coordinates, units, area, volume, scale, and spatial representations.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze polynomial, rational, exponential, logarithmic, and trigonometric functions as preparation for continuous mathematics.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Use code, symbolic and numerical tools, visualization, testing, and reproducible experiments to investigate mathematics responsibly.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Read and construct proofs using logic, sets, functions, relations, induction, contradiction, counterexamples, and precise quantifiers.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Interpret limits, continuity, derivatives, approximation, and optimization across symbolic, graphical, numerical, and applied forms.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Reason with finite structures, counting, recurrence, graphs, algorithms, invariants, and discrete proof methods.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Use vectors, matrices, linear maps, subspaces, eigenstructure, inner products, and decompositions to solve and explain linear problems.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Connect accumulation and change through integration, differential equations, parametric curves, sequences, series, and approximation.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Differentiate and integrate scalar and vector fields in several variables and interpret the major integral theorems.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Model uncertainty with random variables, conditioning, independence, distributions, expectation, transforms, and limit reasoning.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Classify, solve, approximate, and interpret ordinary differential equations and linear systems while checking model assumptions.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Carry out an ethical statistical investigation from question and study design through estimation, testing, uncertainty, and communication.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Formulate, analyze, validate, compare, and communicate models while making assumptions, scales, sensitivity, and limitations explicit.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Prove and apply the foundations of limits, continuity, differentiation, integration, sequences, series, and metric-space reasoning.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Reason structurally with groups, rings, fields, homomorphisms, quotients, actions, and polynomial constructions.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Use structural, enumerative, extremal, algebraic, and algorithmic methods on graphs and finite configurations.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Prove and compute with divisibility, congruences, Diophantine equations, arithmetic functions, quadratic residues, and prime structure.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze holomorphic functions through complex differentiation, contour integration, series, residues, conformal maps, and harmonic links.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Work with topological spaces, continuity, products, quotients, compactness, connectedness, separation, and elementary homotopy.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Compare Euclidean, affine, projective, spherical, hyperbolic, and transformation geometries through invariants and constructions.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Relate major mathematical ideas to their historical development, foundational debates, proof cultures, and changing standards of rigor.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze phase portraits, stability, bifurcations, oscillations, maps, chaos, and qualitative behavior of nonlinear systems.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Classify and solve canonical partial differential equations using characteristics, separation, Fourier methods, energy, and boundary data.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Design and assess numerical algorithms using approximation, conditioning, stability, convergence, error bounds, and reproducible experiments.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Formulate and solve linear, convex, constrained, and smooth optimization problems using duality, optimality conditions, and algorithms.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Model and solve network, matching, flow, scheduling, integer, and combinatorial optimization problems with exact and approximate methods.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze discrete- and continuous-time random processes, Markov chains, Poisson processes, martingales, queues, and long-run behavior.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Build, diagnose, interpret, and communicate regression and multivariate models with principled uncertainty and validation.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze formal languages, deduction, semantics, computability, undecidability, incompleteness, and the limits of formal methods.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Study curves, surfaces, manifolds, tangent structures, metrics, curvature, differential forms, and geometric integration.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Quantify information, compression, channel capacity, divergence, coding limits, and statistical decision tradeoffs.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Construct and analyze error-correcting codes and classical cryptosystems using finite algebra, number theory, probability, and adversarial models.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze strategic interaction, equilibrium, bargaining, voting, allocation, incentives, and the assumptions behind social-choice results.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Build and critique dynamical, stochastic, spatial, and network models of biological systems across scales.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Model interest, contingent claims, portfolios, insurance losses, dependence, and risk while stating market and actuarial assumptions.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Reason with sigma-algebras, measures, measurable functions, Lebesgue integration, convergence theorems, product measures, and signed measures.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze normed, Banach, and Hilbert spaces and bounded, compact, and spectral operators with applications to analysis.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Develop probability on measure spaces through conditional expectation, modes of convergence, laws of large numbers, and central limit theory.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Develop groups, rings, modules, fields, tensor constructions, canonical forms, and Galois theory at graduate depth.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Use ideals, localization, modules, chain conditions, dimension, primary decomposition, and homological tools in commutative rings.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Compute and interpret fundamental groups, covering spaces, homology, cohomology, products, and exact sequences as topological invariants.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Use transversality, degree, intersection, Morse ideas, vector bundles, and elliptic viewpoints to derive global conclusions.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Reason with categories, universal properties, adjunctions, limits, abelian categories, complexes, derived functors, and spectral sequences.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Study affine and projective varieties and schemes through coordinate rings, morphisms, sheaves, dimension, smoothness, and cohomological ideas.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze symmetries through group and algebra representations, characters, modules, decomposition, induction, and structural examples.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Connect continuous symmetry, manifolds, Lie algebras, exponential maps, roots, weights, and representations.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Study primes and arithmetic functions using Dirichlet series, complex methods, sieves, exponential sums, and asymptotic reasoning.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze number fields, rings of integers, ideals, ramification, valuations, local fields, and class and unit structures.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Use probabilistic, extremal, algebraic, and asymptotic methods to prove existence and structure in large discrete systems.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Study axiomatic set theory, ordinals, cardinals, choice, models, compactness, types, and independence phenomena.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze functions and operators through Fourier series and transforms, convolution, maximal estimates, distributions, and function spaces.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Treat weak solutions, distributions, Sobolev spaces, elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic equations, regularity, and variational methods.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Work with martingales, Brownian motion, stochastic integration, stochastic differential equations, change of measure, and diffusion generators.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze prediction, regularization, generalization, resampling, sparsity, kernels, ensembles, and high-dimensional uncertainty.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Analyze state-space systems, stability, controllability, observability, feedback, estimation, optimal control, and robustness.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Discretize and solve differential equations with finite difference, finite element, spectral, iterative, adaptive, and scalable methods.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Use variational principles, symmetries, conservation laws, spectral methods, and field equations across continuum and quantum models.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Enter literature on rational and integral points, elliptic curves, heights, reduction, moduli, and arithmetic invariants.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Enter literature connecting curvature, geometric variational problems, elliptic and parabolic PDE, flows, compactness, and singularities.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Enter literature on persistent homology, shape summaries, manifold methods, stability, algorithms, and statistical validation for data.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Enter literature on identifiability, regularization, Bayesian inversion, sensitivity, uncertainty propagation, and computational inference.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Enter literature on random, temporal, and multilayer networks, spreading, synchronization, emergence, inference, and intervention.

decks.subject: mathematics
decks.description: Enter literature on quantum states, channels, entanglement, entropy, coding, algorithms, and operator-theoretic limits.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Define systems and quantities; measure, estimate, graph, scale, model, and check physical claims.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Plan introductory investigations, calibrate instruments, propagate uncertainty, analyze data, and defend conclusions.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Translate physical models into tested code, visualize results, assess numerical error, and extract physical insight.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Model motion, forces, momentum, energy, rotation, gravitation, and oscillation with conservation and checks.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Predict propagation, interference, diffraction, sound, imaging, polarization, and spectra across representations.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Relate state variables, energy transfer, entropy, cycles, kinetic models, and limits of equilibrium reasoning.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Analyze charge, DC and AC circuit elements, electric fields, potential, capacitance, and energy.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Connect magnetic interactions, induction, Maxwell structure, electromagnetic waves, radiation, and circuits.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Use events, frames, invariants, spacetime diagrams, causal structure, and relativistic energy-momentum.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Interpret quantization, wave-particle evidence, simple wavefunctions, spectra, and quantum structure of matter.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Use generalized coordinates, variational principles, Hamiltonian structure, phase portraits, stability, and chaos.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Model stress, strain, conservation, flow, viscosity, vorticity, waves, instabilities, and scaling in continua.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Move between microstates and macrostates using ensembles, partition functions, fluctuations, phases, and transport foundations.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Solve boundary-value, field, wave, radiation, and relativistic electrodynamics problems with Maxwell theory.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Use states, operators, symmetries, angular momentum, identical particles, approximation, and scattering methods.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Design open-ended experiments; build and troubleshoot instruments; handle signals, noise, controls, and reproducible reports.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Reason with lattices, reciprocal space, bands, quasiparticles, magnetism, superconductivity, and collective phases.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Model atomic and molecular structure, spectra, collisions, selection rules, and light-matter interactions.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Interpret nuclear structure, decay, reactions, symmetries, energetics, detectors, and model limits.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Infer stellar, galactic, compact-object, and high-energy processes from dynamics, radiation, spectra, and observations.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Translate among single-particle, kinetic, fluid, wave, instability, confinement, and space-plasma models.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Explain mesoscale structure, Brownian motion, rheology, polymers, colloids, liquid crystals, and self-assembly.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Connect electronic structure, bonding, spectroscopy, molecular motion, reaction dynamics, and statistical rate models.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Use scaling, mechanics, transport, stochasticity, energetics, and information to model living systems.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Explain imaging, radiation interactions, dosimetry, therapy, reconstruction, uncertainty, safety, and clinical model limits.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Analyze radiation balance, rotating fluids, transport, feedback, variability, observations, and model uncertainty.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Interpret particles, symmetries, interactions, conservation rules, accelerators, detectors, and Standard Model evidence.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Relate geometry, stress-energy, field equations, weak fields, black holes, orbital tests, and gravitational radiation.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Connect expansion, thermal history, perturbations, nucleosynthesis, structure, observations, and parameter inference.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Analyze resonators, gain, coherence, photon statistics, open systems, nonlinear optics, and coherent control.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Analyze states, channels, entanglement, measurement, error correction, algorithms, and physical platforms.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Analyze beam optics, collective effects, acceleration, sources, detectors, calibration, and event inference.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Develop fields, symmetries, quantization, amplitudes, renormalization, gauge theory, and Standard Model structure.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Analyze stochastic dynamics, response, transport, pattern formation, networks, emergence, and driven matter.

decks.subject: physics
decks.description: Enter literature on sources, detectors, signals, inference, populations, and joint electromagnetic, particle, and gravitational observations.
